title: lathyrus albus var. pannonicus
intro: Lathyrus albus var. pannonicus, also known as the White Pea Vetch or Hungarian Pea Vetch, is a perennial legume native to Europe, characterized by its white flowers and white seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. Consuming the plant in moderation has been associated with various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced antioxidant defenses. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus albus var. pannonicus can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used to treat conditions such as urinary tract infections, digestive issues, and skin problems.
